title: Termite
extract: Termites are a group of eusocial Cockroaches which consume a variety of decaying plant material, generally in the form of wood, leaf litter, and soil humus. They are distinguished by their beaded (moniliform) antennae and the soft-bodied, unpigmented worker caste for which they have been commonly termed "white ants"; however, they are not ants but highly derived cockroaches; they are genetically closer to some cockroach groups than these are to other cockroaches. About 2,997 extant species are currently described, 2,125 of which are members of the family Termitidae.
extract_html: Termites are a group of eusocial Cockroaches which consume a variety of decaying plant material, generally in the form of wood, leaf litter, and soil humus. About 2,997 extant species are currently described, 2,125 of which are members of the family Termitidae.
